title: Harden fact_procurement inputs — NULL date_key rows leak into the fact, unknown units silently pass through as kg, unitpriceeur semantics unverified
description: Three input-hardening gaps in the fact_procurement build. (A) Rows whose date fails the cast keep NULL date_key via the left join to dim_date — unlike material/country misses there is no placeholder or filter, so in DirectLake those rows vanish from every date-filtered visual while still counting in unfiltered totals; card totals and time-series won't reconcile, and no RI check covers date_key. (B) unit_norm covers only kg/g/mg/t (matching calculations.md) but the fallback for any OTHER unit is .otherwise(quantity) — a row with unit 'lb' or 'tonne' keeps its raw magnitude labeled kg, silently corrupting quantity_base and spend_eur, with no DQ check on the unit domain. (C) calculations.md asserts unitprice_eur is EUR-per-kg; bronze carries a per-row Unit column, so if the source price is actually per row-unit, spend for tonne-denominated rows is inflated x1000. The audit could not verify this against the Azure SQL source — it needs a one-time human confirmation.
acceptanceCriteria: NULL date_key rows are handled deliberately: either quarantined to the unmapped audit (consistent with the material/country placeholder philosophy — 'no data loss' medallion principle) or given an explicit unknown-date member in dim_date; silently-vanishing-from-date-visuals is eliminated. Choice commented in the notebook.

Unknown units no longer default to kg: rows with units outside unit_norm are flagged (quarantine/audit row + warning) instead of passing through unconverted (fix silver-to-gold2:1097-1101). The distinct unit domain observed in silver is printed/logged each run.

[Erik] unitpriceeur semantics confirmed against the Azure SQL source (is the price per kg or per the row's Unit?) and the answer recorded in calculations.md and bronze_tables.md. If per-row-unit, the spend formula is corrected to quantity_original x price (no base conversion on the price side) and historical spend is rebuilt.
A unit-domain DQ check and a date_key RI/null check are added or delegated to task-026's check list (cross-reference, don't duplicate — note which task carries them).
After /run-gold: COUNT of fact_procurement rows with NULL date_key is 0 (or they are all represented in the audit/quarantine), and unfiltered vs date-sliced spend totals reconcile in the report.
notes: AUDIT-DERIVED (pipeline quality audit 2026-07-13): findings #13, #15 (medium tier). Not spec-decomposed — empty drift fingerprints.

EVIDENCE (A): txn_date cast + left join to dim_date_lu at silver-to-gold2:1076,1087; final select keeps d.date_key nullable at 1141; placeholder handling exists for material/country (1122-1138) but not date. RI checks in data_quality_checks (712-718) omit dim_date entirely.

EVIDENCE (B): unit_norm map at lines 1065-1071 (kg/g/mg/t only, comment '... (rest remains same)'); fallback .otherwise(F.col('p.quantity')) at 1099-1101. calculations.md:14-18 documents exactly these four factors, so the map matches the doc — the FALLBACK is the bug.

EVIDENCE (C): calculations.md:7 'spend_eur = quantity_base (kg) x unitprice_eur (EUR/kg)'; bronze schema has Unit per row (bronze_tables.md:12) making per-unit pricing plausible. UNVERIFIED in audit — flagged explicitly as needing source confirmation, hence owner=both.

SIDE NOTE for consistency: populate_low_confidence_audit computes spend_impact as SUM(quantity * unitpriceeur) (line 2325) — i.e. WITHOUT unit conversion — inconsistent with fact spend (quantity_base * price). Whichever semantics (C) confirms, align this too.
COORDINATE: same file as tasks 023/025/027/028 — serialize.

FR-006 REVERSE-LINK 2026-07-23: this task owns the register entry for the silver<->gold spend reconciliation check (data_quality_framework.md § 2.3 Check 8). That check reconciles exactly only under uniform base units — gold computes spend_eur from kg-normalized quantity_base while unitprice_eur is not converted — so task-026 classified it ADVISORY rather than blocking. It can be promoted to blocking once AC3 confirms the unitprice basis (and, if the answer is 'per row unit', once UNITPRICE_BASIS is flipped). FR-006 therefore closes with AC3, not with the code already shipped.

AC3 RESOLVED 2026-07-23 (per_row_unit) via live-source measurement (Playwright read of a Fabric notebook). Unit domain = {kg:108, pcs:24}; no tonnes/grams. pcs = electronic control units + tyres, priced per piece → UnitPriceEUR is per-row-unit. CRITICAL: the shipped per_kg formula (spend = quantity_base × price) NULLed every pcs row's spend because quantity_base is NULL for non-mass units — €1,743,318.52 of real procurement (the largest category) silently lost. Fixed: UNITPRICE_BASIS='per_row_unit' → spend = quantity × unitprice for all rows (kg spend unchanged; pcs spend restored; quantity_base stays NULL for pcs, correctly). AC2 audit/warning text updated (a non-mass unit withholds MASS, not SPEND). Docs aligned. AC3's original protocol prose had the interpretation inverted (verify-agent only covered AC1/AC2/AC4) — corrected in the protocol steps. AC5 (post-run reconciliation) is the only remaining item; run gold to rebuild spend.

AC3/AC5 SPEND VERIFIED ON LIVE DATA 2026-07-23 (Playwright read of a Fabric notebook grid, after Update-from-git + full silver-to-gold2 run). fact_procurement by unit class: no-mass/pcs = 24 rows, 0 NULL spend, €1,743,318.52; mass/kg = 108 rows, 0 NULL spend, €1,530,457.51. The €1.74M pcs spend is restored, zero NULL-spend rows in either class, kg spend unchanged. NOTE: pcs is 53% of total procurement spend by value (€1.74M of €3.27M) — the per_kg bug was hiding the MAJORITY of spend, not just the largest category. The migration guard added to silver-to-gold2 (commit 96ccbe5) let the whole gold notebook complete end-to-end despite the un-migrated silver_wgi (task-035 still pending). REMAINING for AC5: (a) date_key=0-NULL holds by construction via AC1's sentinel (verified locally; not re-checked live, and note the date_key RI check lives in data_quality_checks which was NOT run here); (b) the 'unfiltered vs date-sliced spend reconcile in the report' check is a Power BI step that needs the semantic model current — folded into task-034.

verification_history.summary: AC1/AC2/AC4 verified by EXECUTION over notebook source extracted verbatim by line range - 33/33 local Spark assertions, 94/94 pytest. BOUNDARY CLAIM CORRECTED: pre-change F.min ignores NULLs so the old boundary was the real window min (20240315), NOT a table wipe - there was no pre-existing data-loss bug. The 19000101 collapse is a hazard AC1's sentinel INTRODUCES; the shipped guard pre-empts it and leaves task-024 semantics unchanged (guarded boundary == pre-change boundary; sentinel rows sit below it so undated rows survive a full load). AC1: 0 NULL date_key reach the fact, sentinel exists exactly once (the != filter de-dups even when the calendar covers 1900-01-01), attributes reproduced by an independent call to the shared date_attributes() helper, LEFT ANTI fact->dim = 0 orphans so task-026's BLOCKING RI check stays green by construction, row count preserved 8/8. AC2: 'lb' -> NULL quantity_base AND NULL spend_eur, NULL unit likewise, padded '  KG ' still converts (TRIM guard genuinely prevents a NEW null), audit persisted to its OWN gold_unmapped_unit_audit. Gap-registry containment confirmed with positive control: populate_gap_registry reads only the two audit tables by explicit name; the new table appears nowhere in it. AC4: BLOCKING_CHECKS byte-identical to baseline, new rule advisory-only, date_key check delegated to task-026's existing ri_checks entry not duplicated. The implementer's self-caught NULL-semantics bug reproduced and the guard confirmed. Scope clean: all silver-to-gold2 hunks inside old 1109-1388, zero hunks in task-028's work (1650+) or the registry region (2473+). Fabric cell-kind sequence identical to baseline in both notebooks, every META payload valid JSON.

issues.description: The completion report framed the boundary guard as fixing a pre-existing latent data-loss bug; measured behaviour shows F.min ignores NULLs, so the hazard is introduced by the sentinel and correctly pre-empted. Code right, framing wrong.

calculations.md and gold_tables.md still document the pre-change contract (quantity_base always populated, no UNKNOWN-DATE member, no gold_unmapped_unit_audit). Captured as FR-022; belongs to task-033's doc sweep.

NULL quantity_base/spend_eur escape the pre-existing advisory 'Positive Quantity'/'Positive Spend' fact rules (0 violations despite 2 NULL rows) - the same NULL semantics guarded in the new rule was not retro-applied. Coverage not lost: the new silver-side rule catches the population one layer earlier.
unionByName with the sentinel branch relaxes gold_dim_date's Delta schema nullability. Harmless under overwriteSchema=true, no consumer asserts nullability, but the written schema does change.
Three extra Spark actions per gold run; gold_unmapped_unit_audit is window-scoped in incremental mode like the existing audit tables (a no-op today since p_from_date defaults to 1900-01-01).

test_protocol.summary: AC3 (unitpriceeur basis, against Azure SQL) and AC5 (post-/run-gold reconciliation) remain yours; AC1/AC2/AC4 are verified locally.

steps.instruction: RESOLVED 2026-07-23 (per_row_unit). Confirmed against oem_lh.bronze_procurement_transactional: the unit domain is {kg:108, pcs:24} — no mass units other than kg. pcs (electronic control units, tyres) can only be priced per piece, so UnitPriceEUR is per the row's Unit, not per kg. [Historical note: the earlier text here said 'a tonne row priced ~1000x a kg row means per-kg' — that was INVERTED; ~1000x would have meant per-row-unit. Moot now, since there are no tonnes.]

DONE 2026-07-23: UNITPRICE_BASIS set to 'per_row_unit' in silver-to-gold2; calculations.md, bronze_tables.md and gold_tables.md aligned; populate_low_confidence_audit already used the quantity × unitprice form so it is now consistent. Re-run gold to rebuild spend — the 24 pcs rows (€1.74M, incl. the Electronics category) move from NULL back to real spend.
steps.expected: After /run-gold: fact_procurement pcs rows carry real spend_eur; quantity_base still NULL for pcs.

steps.instruction: Run /run-gold, then read the '--- Unit domain observed in silver_procurement ---' block and the date warning line.
steps.expected: The unit table lists the real source vocabulary; either 0 rows on the 19000101 member or an explicit undated count.

steps.instruction: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM oem_lh.fact_procurement WHERE date_key IS NULL; and SELECT * FROM oem_lh.gold_unmapped_unit_audit;
steps.expected: NULL count is 0. The unit audit is empty if the source only uses kg/g/mg/t, else it names each unmapped unit.

steps.instruction: In Power BI compare the unfiltered total-spend card against the same measure summed across all date-dimension values.
steps.expected: They reconcile. Any residual gap is attributable to unrecognized-unit rows (NULL spend_eur), which the audit names.
